preamble: The transformation of a voiceless palato-alveolar affricate /tʃ/ into a voiced alveolar fricative /z/, typically involving stages of deaffrication and voicing.

examples.note: In Old/Medieval Portuguese, the voiced affricate /dʒ/ could simplify to [ʒ]/[z]; whether the voiceless /tʃ/ directly shifted to [z] (requiring voicing) is less clear. The specific lexical context is not given in the source.

sources: Williams, E. B. (1938). From Latin to Portuguese: Historical Phonology and Morphology of the Portuguese Language. University of Pennsylvania Press.
